description: A value of the wrong type is stored because the column relies on affinity instead of a STRICT table or CHECK.
body: Failure mode: code inserts a string (e.g. '123' or 'N/A') into an INTEGER-affinity column; SQLite's flexible typing stores it as text rather than rejecting it, so later numeric comparisons and aggregates behave unexpectedly. Detection signals: ORDER BY or SUM on a numeric column gives odd results; queries return text where numbers are expected; the table is not declared STRICT and has no CHECK. Do not rely on the declared type to enforce storage. Safer fallback: declare the table STRICT (modern SQLite) or add CHECK(typeof(col)='integer') constraints, and validate/convert values in application code.
